cot_prefix: Okay, let's visualize this step-by-step. I'm facing forward, which I'll assume is facing north. Taking 2 steps right would mean turning right (so now facing east) and taking 2 steps. Then taking 4 steps right would mean turning another right (now facing south) and taking 4 steps. Then taking 6 steps left would mean turning left (so now facing east again) and taking 6 steps. Hmm, let me think more carefully about this. Actually, when the instructions say "take X steps right", it could mean either turning right and then taking steps, or taking steps to the right while still facing forward. This is ambiguous. Let me consider both interpretations. Interpretation 1: Turning right and then taking steps. - Start facing north. - Take 2 steps right: turn right to face east and take 2 steps east. - Take 4 steps right: turn right to face south and take 4 steps south. - Take 6 steps left: turn left to face east and take 6 steps east. - Final position is 2 + 6 = 8 steps east and 4 steps south from the starting point. Interpretation 2: Taking steps to the right while facing forward. - Start facing north. - Take 2 steps right: take 2 steps to the right (east). - Take 4 steps right: take 4 steps to the right (east). - Take 6 steps left: take 6 steps to the left (west). - Final position is 2 + 4 - 6 = 0 steps east from the starting point. Wait, I need to be more precise. When facing north, right would be east and left would be west. So 2 steps right = 2 east, 4 steps right = 4 east, 6 steps left = 6 west. So total east-west position is 2+4-6 = 0. North-south position is 0 (never moved north or south). So I would end up back at the starting point.
